description: Electric Boat Corp., Groton, Connecticut, is awarded a $517,262,000 cost-plus-incentive-fee contract for detail design and construction of the second Virginia-class submarine in Block VI, SSN 813. Work will be performed in Groton, Connecticut, and is expected to be completed by December 2035.

Leidos Inc., Reston, Virginia, has been awarded a $389,000,000 indefinite-delivery/indefinite-quantity contract for enterprise IT services supporting Air Force systems sustainment, cybersecurity monitoring, and help desk operations across 42 bases worldwide.

Palantir Technologies Inc., Denver, Colorado, is awarded a $178,400,000 firm-fixed-price contract for the Army Vantage AI/ML data analytics platform, supporting command decision support, logistics optimization, and predictive maintenance across Army enterprise systems.

BWX Technologies Inc., Lynchburg, Virginia, is awarded an $875,000,000 cost-plus-fixed-fee contract for nuclear reactor components and fuel fabrication services for the Navy's fleet of nuclear-powered submarines and aircraft carriers. Sole-source award under 10 U.S.C. 3204(a)(1).

Booz Allen Hamilton Inc., McLean, Virginia, has been awarded a $295,000,000 IDIQ contract for intelligence analysis support, data exploitation, and machine learning model development to support national intelligence priorities.

Kratos Defense & Security Solutions Inc., San Diego, California, is awarded a $68,500,000 firm-fixed-price contract for tactical unmanned aerial target drone systems (XQ-58A Valkyrie production lot 3) in support of adversarial air combat training and next-generation fighter development programs.

Humana Government Business Inc., Louisville, Kentucky, exercised a $9,800,000,000 option on the TRICARE East Region managed care support contract, providing comprehensive healthcare services for approximately 2.7 million active duty military, retirees, and their families across 28 eastern states.

Lockheed Martin Corp., Bethesda, Maryland, is awarded a $1,200,000,000 modification to a previously awarded contract for Terminal High Altitude Area Defense (THAAD) interceptor production and technical data package updates. Work includes advanced interceptor hardware, software integration, and worldwide logistics support.

Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc., Waltham, Massachusetts, is awarded a $142,000,000 firm-fixed-price contract for next-generation biological threat detection instruments, field portable mass spectrometers, and laboratory diagnostic platforms supporting CBRN threat response and readiness.

CrowdStrike Inc., Austin, Texas, is awarded a $191,000,000 firm-fixed-price contract for endpoint detection and response (EDR) and extended detection and response (XDR) cybersecurity platform services across DoD enterprise networks, covering approximately 1.4 million endpoints.

Science Applications International Corporation (SAIC), Reston, Virginia, is awarded a $224,000,000 IDIQ for software development, systems engineering, and cybersecurity support for Navy command, control, communications, computers, and intelligence (C4I) systems.

Northrop Grumman Systems Corp., Falls Church, Virginia, is awarded a $2,100,000,000 modification for continued development and low-rate initial production of the B-21 Raider long-range strike bomber under the Engineering Manufacturing and Development contract. Modification includes additional test aircraft and initial production articles.

Accenture Federal Services LLC, Arlington, Virginia, has been awarded a $312,000,000 IDIQ for enterprise resource planning (ERP) modernization, supply chain optimization, and digital transformation services supporting Defense Logistics Agency global supply chain management systems.

Rocket Lab USA Inc., Long Beach, California, is awarded a $23,900,000 firm-fixed-price contract for two Electron launch vehicle missions to deliver Space Development Agency Tranche 2 Transport Layer satellites to low Earth orbit.

Parsons Corporation, Centreville, Virginia, is awarded a $445,000,000 firm-fixed-price contract for electronic warfare systems integration, directed energy countermeasures, and base defense infrastructure hardening across Army installations in Europe and the Indo-Pacific.

Sunrise Medical Laboratories LLC, Hauppauge, New York, is awarded an $18,400,000 firm-fixed-price contract for clinical laboratory testing services for the Military Treatment Facility at Fort Hamilton, New York.

Huntington Ingalls Industries Inc., Newport News, Virginia, is awarded a $3,800,000,000 cost-plus-incentive-fee contract modification for continued construction and test activities on CVN-80 Enterprise aircraft carrier and advance construction work on CVN-81 Doris Miller. The aircraft carrier program represents a sole-source, strategic national asset with no domestic alternative supplier.

RTX Corporation (Raytheon Missiles & Defense), Tucson, Arizona, is awarded a $1,850,000,000 firm-fixed-price contract for production of 400 Patriot Advanced Capability-3 Missile Segment Enhancement interceptors for the U.S. Army and FMS customers including Poland, Romania, and Japan. This is a multi-year procurement contract.

L3Harris Technologies Inc., Melbourne, Florida, is awarded a $680,000,000 indefinite-delivery/indefinite-quantity contract for modernized tactical communications systems including AN/PRC-163 multi-channel handheld radios, waveform upgrades, and software-defined radio integration for Army, Marine Corps, and Special Operations Forces.

CACI International Inc., Arlington, Virginia, is awarded a $465,000,000 IDIQ contract for intelligence analysis support, signals intelligence exploitation, and targeting support for Army Intelligence and Security Command (INSCOM) operations. Work includes linguist support, SIGINT analysis, and counter-IED intelligence production.

AeroVironment Inc., Arlington, Virginia, is awarded a $320,000,000 firm-fixed-price contract for Switchblade 600 loitering munitions systems and Puma 3 AE small unmanned aircraft systems for the U.S. Army and Special Operations Command. Production covers 1,400 Switchblade units and 200 Puma systems with associated ground control stations.
